name: Budgetly
when: The one hospitality competitor with genuine feature parity — comes up in almost every serious evaluation
angle: The real like-for-like competitor: individual cards, instant issuance and point-of-sale budget enforcement all match on paper. Differentiate on execution and receipt-nudge reliability — and note that at least one hospitality customer evaluated Weel ahead of Budgetly and chose Weel.

name: ProSpend
when: Comes up in formal multi-vendor evaluations, often alongside Concur
angle: Strong across budgets, approvals and reporting, but card issuance and instant issuance are partial/add-on rather than native. Worth probing exactly how "instant" a replacement card really is in practice.

name: SAP Concur
when: Evaluated by larger multi-venue groups doing a formal software review
angle: Enterprise-grade expense management bolted onto an existing bank card, not a native card issuer — no individual card issuance, no instant issuance, and no way to enforce a budget at the point of sale.

name: FlexiPurchase
status: Expense tool, not a card issuer
when: Comes up when a group has looked at receipt/expense software rather than a card platform
angle: No individual card issuance, no instant issuance, no per-venue real-time visibility, and no point-of-sale budget enforcement — sits closer to a receipt-management tool than a spend-control platform.

name: Webexpenses
when: Surfaces alongside other UK-origin expense tools in a software review
angle: An expense-management add-on, not a card issuer — no individual cards, no point-of-sale enforcement, so it can only ever report on spend after it's already happened.

name: Airwallex
status: Strong on payments, weak on cards
when: Comes up with fast-growing, multi-entity groups already using Airwallex for FX or payments
angle: Solid multi-entity visibility and reporting, but doesn't issue individually attributable cards the way Weel does. At least one fast-growing multi-entity franchise group evaluated Weel head-to-head against Airwallex — specifically on accounts payable functionality — and chose Weel.
